raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Assembly passed AB 1414 with a 72-0 vote.",
    "AB 1414 ensures that renters in California are not forced by landlords to use a specific internet service provider (ISP).",
    "AB 1414 allows tenants the freedom to choose the broadband service that best fits their needs and budget.",
    "High-quality internet connections are essential for work, school, healthcare, and daily life.",
    "AB 1414 protects renters from being locked into costly or inadequate internet plans.",
    "AB 1414 promotes a competitive market for internet service providers.",
    "If a landlord imposes a required ISP on tenants, renters can deduct that cost from their rent.",
    "AB 1414 closes a gap in California law regarding exclusive ISP agreements in multi-unit buildings.",
    "AB 1414 aligns with guidance from the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)."
  ]
}
```
